title: cuscuta breviflora subsp. rogowitschiana
intro: Cuscuta breviflora subsp. rogowitschiana is a subspecies of the dodder plant, a type of parasitic plant that obtains its nutrients by connecting itself to the tissues of other plants. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory activities, making it useful for treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Cuscuta breviflora subsp. rogowitschiana include its potential to improve fertility, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in combination with other herbs, and is typically available in the form of capsules, tablets, or teas.
